pattern: Sort both arrays and compare minimums to recover the uniform shift applied to all elements.
hints: Since every element is shifted by the same constant x, sorting both arrays preserves the mapping: the i-th smallest in nums1 maps to the i-th smallest in nums2.
The value of x equals the difference between the minimum of nums2 and the minimum of nums1 (or equivalently min(nums2) - min(nums1)).
Verify the answer by confirming sorted(nums1)[i] + x == sorted(nums2)[i] for all i, though the problem guarantees a valid x exists.

edge_inputs.description: maximum values with shift reducing to 0

stress.description: random arrays with valid uniform shift
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    x = rng.randint(-500, 500)
    nums1 = [rng.randint(0, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    # ensure all nums2 values in [0,1000]
    shift = max(-min(nums1), x) if x < 0 else x
    # just use x that keeps everything in range
    base = [rng.randint(max(0, -x), min(1000, 1000 - x)) for _ in range(n)]
    x2 = rng.randint(0, 500)
    nums1b = [rng.randint(0, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2b = [v + x2 for v in nums1b]
    if all(0 <= v <= 1000 for v in nums2b):
        return [nums1b, nums2b]
    nums1c = [rng.randint(0, 500) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums1c, nums1c]
